publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 22:09

did.unittitle: Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission publications

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: The collection includes publications and reprints about health and genetic effects of radiation exposure or about the Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission (ABCC) published from 1952-1975. The majority of items were published by the ABCC, but the collection also includes items published by Japanese govenment entities and authors associated with the ABCC or Japan National Institute of Health (JNIH). The ABCC was created in 1946 by the United States National Research Council, Division of Medical Sciences, to conduct a long-term study of the health effects of radiation on the survivors of the atomic bombings of the cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki during World War II. ABCC topics of research included hematology, radiation cataracts, incidence of leukemia and other cancers, genitourinary system disorders, aging and mortality rates, sex ratios and mortality rates of survivors' children, and genetics. Most materials are in English, with a few items written in Japanese and English.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance of items in the collection is unknown.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized by date and author.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission (ABCC) was formed in 1946 in response to a directive by United States President Harry S. Truman to study the long-term health effects of radiation on the survivors of the atomic bombings of the cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki during World War II. The ABCC was formed under the National Research Council's Division of Medical Sciences and initially consisted of five people who were sent to Japan to conduct a preliminary survey. In 1947 the ABCC was officially established to conduct a long-term study of survivors' medical and genetic health in the field. The ABCC worked in partnership with the Japan National Institute of Health (JNIH) and by 1950 employed more than 1,000 people in Japan, the majority of whom were Japanese citizens. The commission did not provide health care to survivors or their children.

The ABCC continued its' studies until 1975, when it was absorbed into the newly formed Radiation Effects Research Foundation, a private non-profit foundation jointly funded by the governments of the United States and Japan.

The ABCC's topics of research included hematology, radiation cataracts, incidence of leukemia and other cancers, genitourinary system disorders, aging and mortality rates, sex ratios and mortality rates of survivors' children, genetics, and other health factors impacted by radiation exposure. The commission published numerous reports, which were reprinted in scientific journals, and scientists working for the ABCC and JNIH often published their own articles and reports related to ABCC research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission publications. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Averill E. Liebow collection (Ms Coll 28), Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes publications and reprints about health and genetic effects of radiation exposure or about the Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission (ABCC) published from 1952-1975. The majority of items were published by the ABCC, but the collection also includes items published by Japanese govenment entities and authors associated with the ABCC or Japan National Institute of Health (JNIH). The ABCC was created in 1946 by the United States National Research Council, Division of Medical Sciences, to conduct a long-term study of the health effects of radiation on the survivors of the atomic bombings of the cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki during World War II. ABCC topics of research included hematology, radiation cataracts, incidence of leukemia and other cancers, genitourinary system disorders, aging and mortality rates, sex ratios and mortality rates of survivors' children, and genetics. Most materials are in English, with a few items written in Japanese and English.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Japanese Government, Ministry of Health and Welfare, A summary account of the development of health centers and their present status . 1952.
Japanese Government, Ministry of Health and Welfare, A brief report on public health administration in Japan . 1953.
Harris, Carl A., The Atomic Bomb Casualty Committee , New Report , vol. iv, no. 3. 1954.
Arakawa, E. T., Radiation dosimetry in Hiroshima and Nagasaki atomic-bomb survivors , reprinted from the New England Journal of Medicine , vol. 263, September 8, 1960. 1960.
Darling, George B., ABCC section in Hiroshima Igaku , vol. xiii, no. 11-12. 1960.
Darling, George B., ABCC section in Hiroshima Igaku , ABCC Section no. 1, vol. xiii, no. 11-12. 1960.

Hollingsworth, J. W., Special article: delayed radiation effects in survivors of the atomic bombings: review of the findings of the Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission , 1947-1959, reprinted from the New England Journal of Medicine , vol. 263, September 8, 1960. 1960.
Tokyo Metropolitan Government, Activities of Health Bureau in 1960 . Tokyo, Japan, circa 1960.
Switzer, Sam, Bacteriuria in a healthy population and its relation to hypertension and pyelonephritis , reprinted from the New England Journal of Medicine , vol. 264, January 5, 1961. 1961.
Hollingsworth, James W., Howard B. Hamilton, Changes in red cell membrane , ABCC Technical Reports , Technical Report 1-62. 1962.
Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission, Editorial Office, List of ABCC technical reports , 1959-1963. 1964.

Zeldis, L.J., S. Jablon, M. Ishida, Current status of ABCC-NIH studies of carcinogenesis in Hiroshima and Nagasaki , reprinted from Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences , vol. 114, article 1, March 31, 1964. 1964.
Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission, "Chart showing the structure of the Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission". 1965.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large chart with text in English and Japanese.

did.unittitle: Freedman, Lawrence B., William G. Blackard, Leonard A. Sagan, Morihiro Ishida, Howard B. Hamilton, The epidemiology of diabetes mellitus in Hiroshima and Nagasaki , reprinted from the Yale Journal of Biology and Medicine , vol. 37, no. 4, February 1965. 1965.

Freedman, Lawrence R., John P. Phair, Masafumi Seki, Howard B. Hamilton, M. Dean Nefzger, The epidemiology of urinary tract infections in Hiroshima , reprinted from the Yale Journal of Biology and Medicine , vol. 37, no. 4, February 1965. 1965.

Bloom, Arthur D., Shotaro Neriishi, Nanao Kamada, Testsuya Iseki, Robert J. Keehn, Cytogenetic investigation of survivors of the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki , reprinted from The Lancet , September 24, 1966. 1966.

Bizzozero, O. Joseph, Kenneth G. Johnson, Antonio Ciocco, Sho Kawaski, Shigeri Toyoda, Radiation-related leukemia in Hiroshima and Nagaski 1946-1964, ii. observations on type-specific leukemia, survivorship, and clinical behavior , reprinted from the Annals of Internal Medicine , vol. 66, no. 3, March 1967. 1967.

Bloom, Arthur D., Masanori Tsuchioka, Takeshi Wajima, Lactic dehydronase and metabolism of human leukocytes in vitro , reprinted from Science , May 19, 1967, vol. 156, no. 3777. 1967.

Freedman, Lawrence R., M. Seki, John P. Phair, M.D. Nefzger, Proteinuria in Hiroshima and Nagasaki--Japan , reprinted from the Yale Journal of Biology and Medicine , vol. 40, no. 2, October 1967. 1967.

Hoshino, Takashi, Hiroo Kato, Stuart C. Finch, Zdenek Hrubec, Leukemia in off-spring of atomic bomb survivors , reprinted from Blood , vol. 30, no. 6, December 1967. 1967.

Inventory of ABCC autopsies: Hiroshima and Nagasaki, 1965-66 supplement , technical report 1-67 . 1967.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 copies, collection also contains editorial response.

did.unittitle: Editorials: thyroid cancer in Japan , reprinted from the Journal of the American Medical Association , July 7, 1969, vol. 209. 1969.

Keehn, Thyroid carcinoma after exposure to atomic radiation: a continuing survey of a fixed population, Hiroshima and Nagasaki, 1958-1971 , reprinted from Annals of Internal Medicine , vol. 80, no. 5, May 1974. United States, 1974.
